When used correctly, it significantly improves feedback speed and decision-making.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"However, many teams fail to unlock its full potential. The problem is rarely the tool itself – it’s how teams analyze and interpret the data.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Below are the most common mistakes teams make when working with test results in ReportPortal, along with practical advice on how to avoid them.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"1. Analyzing reports without a clear goal","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"A frequent mistake is reviewing reports without a specific purpose. Teams open a dashboard, notice the number of failed tests, and move on.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal provides data, not conclusions. Without clear questions, analysis remains superficial. Ignoring custom defect types","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Many teams rely only on default ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/work-with-reports/InvestigationOfFailure#set-defect-type"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"defect types","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" (Product Bug, Automation Bug, System Issue, No Defect, To Investigate) and never go further. Blind trust in ML classification","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal’s ML features are powerful, but they are not a replacement for process. Some teams either ignore ML entirely or trust it without verification.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Problems occur when:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"training data is inconsistent","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"predictions are not reviewed","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"test logs are unstable","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Regularly confirm or correct ML predictions. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Ensure consistent logging for similar failures. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Treat ML as an assistant, not a decision-maker","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"4. Mixing incompatible test runs in one analysis","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Another common mistake is analyzing tests with different purposes together:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"UI and API tests ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"smoke and regression suites","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This makes metrics misleading. A failure rate acceptable for nightly runs may be unacceptable for smoke tests.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Separate launches by test purpose. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Use ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/work-with-reports/LaunchesTestItemsAttributes"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"attributes","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" or separate projects.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"5. Underestimating the importance of log quality","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal cannot compensate for poor logging. If logs are inconsistent or lack context, analysis becomes manual and slow. Typical issues:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"the same error reported differently","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"missing steps or environment details","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"no clear failure reason","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Standardize test logging.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Log causes, not just symptoms.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Ensure similar failures produce similar messages.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"6. No clear ownership of test result analysis","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"A critical organizational mistake is having \"shared ownership\" of ReportPortal – meaning no ownership at all. When no one is responsible:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"dashboards degrade over time","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"defect classification becomes inconsistent","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"insights are lost","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Assign a clear owner (for example, QA Lead).","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Continuously improve dashboards and metrics.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Treat ReportPortal as a product, not just a log repository.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal is a decision-support tool, not just a reporting system. Most mistakes teams make are process-related, not technical. Teams that define clear analysis goals, invest in defect classification and logging quality, and assign ownership, turn ReportPortal into a powerful driver of quality and efficiency rather than a source of noisy data.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}

title.title: Common mistakes when analyzing test results with ReportPortal

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: ReportPortal is widely used for analyzing automated test results, aggregating logs, classifying failures with ML, and tracking quality trends over time. When used correctly, it significantly improves feedback speed and decision-making.

Scales With Growing Insurance Ecosystems","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"As insurers expand digital channels, introduce new products, or modernize legacy systems, testing complexity grows. title.title: Benefits of ReportPortal for the insurance sector

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: Testing in the insurance sector is inherently complex. Any change – whether a new insurance product, updated pricing rules, regulatory adjustment, or claims workflow enhancement – impacts multiple interconnected systems.

articleBody.raw: {"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Single test runs rarely tell the full story about product quality. A build can fail today and pass tomorrow – but what really matters is what happens over time. Are failures becoming more frequent? Are the same areas breaking again and again? Is product stability improving or slowly degrading?","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"This is where test result trends become far more valuable than isolated results.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"ReportPortal is designed not just to collect test outcomes, but also to support test automation analytics, helping teams analyze regression testing results over time, identify patterns, and make better quality decisions based on real data.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Beyond a single test run","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"heading-2"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"A failed test run answers only one question:","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"\"Is something broken right now?\" It does not answer: ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Is this a new problem or an old one? ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Is the product improving or getting worse? ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Are failures random, or do they follow a pattern? ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"}],"nodeType":"unordered-list"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"When teams focus only on today’s results, they often: ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Fix symptoms instead of causes ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Accept instability as \"normal\" ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"list-item"}],"nodeType":"unordered-list"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Trends change that perspective.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"How to prepare test data for accurate test execution trends","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"heading-2"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Before analyzing trends, test data must be structured correctly.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Two ReportPortal features are critical here:","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"value":"1. Consistent launch names","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Launch names should represent what is being tested, not when it is tested.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"E2E Regression – good example\nE2E_Regression_2025_02_14_18_32 – bad example","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Build numbers, branches, environments, and versions should be stored as attributes, not embedded in launch names. title.title: Test Result Trends: How to Analyze Regression Testing Results Over Time in ReportPortal

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: Single test runs rarely tell the full story about product quality. A build can fail today and pass tomorrow – but what really matters is what happens over time. Are failures becoming more frequent? Are the same areas breaking again and again? Is product stability improving or slowly degrading?
